name: Ascent to Maigmo - Circular from El Balcon de Alicante
name_original: Ascenso al Maigmó — Circular desde El Balcón de Alicante
region_intro: Hiking around Tibi is characterized by trails through the Maigmó mountain range, offering varied terrain from forested paths to rocky ascents. The region features significant landmarks such as the historic Tibi Dam and panoramic viewpoints. Hikers can explore a network of paths that traverse hills and valleys, providing diverse experiences for different ability levels. The landscape includes Mediterranean vegetation and offers views of the surrounding Alicante province. Best hiking trails around Tibi The most popular hiking route is Ascent to Maigmo - Circular from El Balcon de Alicante, a 2.3 miles (3.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 52 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes a significant ascent towards Maigmo. Another top favourite among local hikers is View of the Tibi Dam – Tibi Reservoir loop from Restaurante Maigmó, a moderate 6.6 miles (10.7 km) path. This route offers views of the historic Tibi Dam and reservoir. Local hikers also love the Maigmó Summit – Alicante Balcony loop from el Maigmó, a 1.4 miles (2.2 km) trail leading through the Maigmó area, often completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes. Hiking around Tibi is defined by mountain paths, reservoir views, and Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging ascents. The routes in Tibi are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Tibi's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Tibi?
A: There are over 35 hiking trails around Tibi, offering a diverse range of experiences through the Maigmó mountain range and surrounding landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Tibi?
A: Hikes around Tibi feature varied terrain, from forested paths to rocky ascents within the Maigmó mountain range. You'll encounter Mediterranean vegetation, hills, and valleys, with some routes offering views of the Alicante province.

Q: Are there any easy hiking options in Tibi?
A: Yes, while many trails are moderate or difficult, there is at least one easy hiking route available around Tibi, perfect for a more relaxed outing.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Tibi?
A: Absolutely. Tibi offers 18 difficult hiking routes, including ascents towards Maigmó Summit. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e869967715/ascent-to-maigmo-circular-from-el-balcon-de-alicante" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Maigmó - Circular from El Balcon de Alicante&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 2.3-mile trail with significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the Tibi area?
A: Yes, many routes around Tibi are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19396557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Tibi Dam – Tibi Reservoir loop from Restaurante Maigmó&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 6.6-mile path.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see while hiking near Tibi?
A: Hikers can explore several notable natural features and viewpoints. You might encounter the historic Tibi Dam and reservoir, or reach summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2835932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maigmó Summit&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3994612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Menejador&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2874564" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alicante Balcony&lt;/a&gt; also offers panoramic views.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Tibi suitable for families?
A: While some trails are challenging, there are moderate and easy options that could be suitable for families, depending on their experience and the children's age. It's best to check the difficulty and length of specific routes before heading out.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Tibi?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in natural areas like Tibi, especially if kept on a leash. However, specific regulations can vary, so it's always advisable to check local signage or park rules for any particular trail you plan to visit.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Tibi?
A: The Mediterranean climate of the Alicante province suggests that spring and autumn are ideal for hiking in Tibi, offering pleasant temperatures. Summers can be hot, and winters mild, but always check the local weather forecast before your trip.

Q: Is parking available for hikers in Tibi?
A: Yes, for many popular trailheads and routes around Tibi, parking facilities are available. For instance, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19396557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Tibi Dam – Tibi Reservoir loop&lt;/a&gt; often start from areas with designated parking, such as near Restaurante Maigmó.

Q: How do other hikers rate the trails around Tibi?
A: The hiking trails around Tibi are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain and the scenic views of the Maigmó mountain range and the historic Tibi Dam.

Q: Are there any longer hiking options around Tibi?
A: Yes, Tibi offers several longer routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19396557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Tibi Dam – Tibi Reservoir loop from Restaurante Maigmó&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.6-mile (10.7 km) path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31400291" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maigmó Summit – Alicante Balcony loop from El Maigmó&lt;/a&gt; extends to nearly 5 miles (7.9 km).
tips: Honoring its name, from the balcony you will have the best views of the city of Alicante and the beautiful beaches of the Costa Blanca. A unique and ideal place, although getting there has its price, maximum slopes of between 18-20%, but I assure you that the views will more than reward that physical effort!

With its summit at 1,296 meters high and its proximity to the sea, it allows you to enjoy exceptional panoramic views, being able to see the entire coastal strip from Benidorm to Torrevieja.
 
It is part of a very important forest core and is therefore protected. Native flora and fauna of the Mediterranean mountains reign here, such as the Aleppo pine, of which you can see some exceptional specimens.
